jokes.text: Good news, everyone! Well? What is it? What is what? Oh! Right!
jokes.setup: Farnsworth opens with his signature catchphrase 'Good news, everyone!'
jokes.punchline: He forgets to actually deliver the news, classic absent-minded professor behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth character beat - the forgetful professor who announces good news then forgets to say what it is

jokes.text: Stop asking questions, old man, And we'll slip you an extra five grand That your crew doesn't need to know about.
jokes.setup: Leela wants to know what's in the mysterious crate
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth reveals he was bribed to not ask questions and is hiding money from his crew
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Farnsworth's moral flexibility and casual corruption

jokes.text: Which gang's red, and which gang's blue? Both!
jokes.setup: Logical question about distinguishing between two gangs by their colors
jokes.punchline: Both gangs wear the same colors, making identification impossible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - the whole premise of gang identification becomes meaningless with perfect economy

jokes.text: Does anyone else find this delivery suspicious? Nope. He's too dumb, and I don't care. Well, I'm smart, and I care too much.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if anyone finds the delivery suspicious
jokes.punchline: Perfect character summation - Fry is too dumb, Bender doesn't care, Leela cares too much
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy that efficiently defines three main characters in one exchange

jokes.text: I just need it in case, uh, Fry gets his head stuck in a pot of honey. And in the meantime, if I should happen To bump into the crate and open it... Whoopsie!
jokes.setup: Leela asks for a crowbar for a ridiculous hypothetical Fry situation
jokes.punchline: She's obviously planning to 'accidentally' open the forbidden crate
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke with absurd excuse followed by transparent real motive

jokes.text: Hey, leela, Fry got his head stuck in a pot of honey! Oh, bother. Not now!
jokes.setup: Leela's ridiculous hypothetical excuse from moments earlier
jokes.punchline: It actually happens while she's trying to open the crate, and she's annoyed by the timing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that pays off the setup perfectly - the absurd excuse becomes reality at the worst moment

jokes.text: Alcohol? Tobacco? Firearms? Firearms! That's the one I was secretly hoping for.
jokes.setup: Leela lists contraband possibilities with increasing severity
jokes.punchline: Bender was hoping for the most dangerous option
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Bender's love of violence and chaos

jokes.text: Then pretend they're little human necks. What?! Let me at 'em!
jokes.setup: Leela needs to motivate Bender to bend gun barrels to disable them
jokes.punchline: Suggesting violence against humans instantly motivates him
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in-character - Leela knows exactly how to motivate Bender's violent tendencies

jokes.text: I hope I never see you again.
jokes.setup: Bender staying behind to guard the ship on a dangerous planet
jokes.punchline: Casual farewell expressing hope he never sees his friends again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - antisocial but delivered so casually it's endearing

jokes.text: There might be spiders!
jokes.setup: Discussing the dangerous gang-infested neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Fry's biggest concern is spiders, not violent criminals
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's misplaced priorities and random fears

jokes.text: Leela, this is no time to show me your boobs! Turn your jersey inside out. Oh. Show me again.
jokes.setup: Dangerous situation where they need to flip jerseys to survive
jokes.punchline: Fry misinterprets Leela's urgent clothing adjustment as something sexual, then asks for an encore
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry behavior - misunderstanding urgent situations and being inappropriately horny

jokes.text: Yo, what you doing on our turf? Chill, dawg. Ain't no thang. We cruds. Well, we blips, and we over here.
jokes.setup: Confrontation between gang members
jokes.punchline: Fry attempts street slang awkwardly, revealing they're the wrong gang
jokes.explanation: Fry's awkward attempt at fitting in with gang culture, typical fish-out-of-water comedy

jokes.text: We put up that mirror 'cause this is a blind intersection. It's considerably reduced the number of accidents. It don't do nothing for the murder rate, though.
jokes.setup: Explaining a civic improvement project in gang territory
jokes.punchline: They care about traffic safety but not about murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - criminals who are civic-minded about traffic safety but casual about murder

jokes.text: Well... Excuse me, sir? We're not actually blips or cruds. We're delivery people, here to deliver this crate. So if everything seems to be in order, We'll be on our way, a'ight?
jokes.setup: Fry appears to have soiled himself from fear
jokes.punchline: Leela awkwardly tries to politely extract them from the dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy with Leela trying to be diplomatic in a violent situation

jokes.text: Oh, it's far from a'ight. Now, hold up! It's a'ight, all right? It's a crud, And he's got a gun!
jokes.setup: Confusion over whether the situation is 'alright'
jokes.punchline: Gang members argue about the proper usage of 'a'ight' while armed
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay comedy about linguistic confusion in tense situation

jokes.text: Yo, you just shot your own reflection in the mirror. It was self-defense.
jokes.setup: Gang member shoots at mirror during confrontation
jokes.punchline: He claims self-defense against his own reflection
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic delivered deadpan - perfect economy and absurd reasoning

jokes.text: Giant spiders. I knew it!
jokes.setup: Gang leader mentions giant spiders as the real enemy
jokes.punchline: Fry's earlier spider paranoia was justified
jokes.explanation: Good callback that retroactively validates Fry's seemingly random fear

jokes.text: I'll test it out on that innocent bystander. Ow! Yo, you just shot yourself again!
jokes.setup: Gang member wants to test weapon on bystander
jokes.punchline: Due to bent barrel, he shoots himself instead of the intended target
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff of the bent barrels sabotage, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Look, let's just agree that gang violence Is an important issue with no easy answers. Okay.
jokes.setup: After witnessing chaotic gang warfare
jokes.punchline: Leela offers a diplomatic non-solution that everyone accepts
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on how complex social issues are often handled with meaningless platitudes

jokes.text: Yeah it was! I got bot-jacked!
jokes.setup: Claims nothing was stolen from their ship
jokes.punchline: Bender appears with just his head, having been 'bot-jacked' (carjacked but for robots)
jokes.explanation: Good pun on carjacking with strong visual element of Bender as just a head

jokes.text: They stole everything except my mouth and eyes. I guess they didn't like all my screaming and winking.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining why only his head remains
jokes.punchline: The thieves left his most annoying parts
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - even thieves find Bender's personality insufferable

jokes.text: My head?! Slap me upside it! Ah, home, sweet head.
jokes.setup: Bender's head is found among junk parts
jokes.punchline: He asks to be slapped 'upside the head' and calls his head 'home'
jokes.explanation: Clever play on the phrase 'home sweet home' and 'slap upside the head' given his current state

jokes.text: What if I throw in giant robot head for nice officer? Hmm... Thanks for all your help, officer!
jokes.setup: Criminal trying to bribe police officer to drop charges
jokes.punchline: Officer accepts the bribe of Bender's giant robot head
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about police corruption, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I didn't even know you had a wishbone. I had a lot of magic things.
jokes.setup: Discovering Bender had a wishbone among his parts
jokes.punchline: Bender casually mentions having 'a lot of magic things'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment about robot anatomy with mysterious magical elements

jokes.text: Come in, come in. Close the door. You're letting the smut out.
jokes.setup: Entering what appears to be an adult bookstore or similar establishment
jokes.punchline: Treating 'smut' like it's a physical substance that can escape
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of keeping smut contained like air conditioning

jokes.text: This ain't a church, lady. Except for tax purposes.
jokes.setup: Response to someone (likely Leela) being shocked by explicit content
jokes.punchline: Reveals the adult shop claims to be a church for tax evasion
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on religious tax exemptions with good economy and timing

jokes.text: All I can give you is this naked picture of him Taken from my toilet cam.
jokes.setup: Refusing to give customer information for privacy reasons
jokes.punchline: Offers secretly recorded nude photos from bathroom surveillance instead
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation joke about privacy violation, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: It has done everything and everyone imaginable. You may want to get it disinfected.
jokes.setup: Senator returning Bender's 'antenna' (euphemism)
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of extensive sexual use requiring disinfection
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy with deadpan delivery, though somewhat predictable sexual humor

jokes.text: It's my body. Oh, come on, bender! Can you at least wait till it's empty? Fair enough. Fry, beer me.
jokes.setup: Party-goers upset about losing their keg
jokes.punchline: The keg is Bender's torso, and he agrees to let them finish drinking him first
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist comedy with Bender's body being used as party equipment

jokes.text: I'm 40% back, baby!
jokes.setup: Bender getting another body part back
jokes.punchline: Parody of his usual '40% [something]' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Bender's running gag adapted to his current situation

jokes.text: But those are my arms! That's gropey, and that's cheaty. Cheaty?!
jokes.setup: Poker player using Bender's robot arms for cheating and inappropriate touching
jokes.punchline: Bender names his arms 'Gropey' and 'Cheaty' and the player is offended by the cheating accusation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with Bender naming his arms based on their misuse, plus great reaction

jokes.text: It's a miracle! I can walk! No more crutch or withered leg! Someone's sure to adopt me now!
jokes.setup: Robot child getting leg transplant surgery
jokes.punchline: His joy about walking is primarily because he thinks it will help him get adopted
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about orphan robot's desperate desire for adoption, mixing joy with sadness

jokes.text: I guess it's back to the cart for me. Ooh, nice cart.
jokes.setup: Bender takes back his legs, leaving Tinny disabled again
jokes.punchline: Tinny graciously compliments Bender's wheelchair despite being devastated
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny moment showing Tinny's resilience and politeness despite cruel circumstances

jokes.text: I'm still missing the shiniest metal piece of all. Your thyroid? No! I'm talking about my ass!
jokes.setup: Bender talking about his missing shiniest part
jokes.punchline: Fry's medical guess is wrong - it's Bender's rear end
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Fry's innocent medical guess vs. Bender's vanity about his posterior

jokes.text: My ass! Sweet love of my life! Several hundred people died, too, Including my wife. My ass!
jokes.setup: Learning Bender's rear end was lost in a tragic shipwreck
jokes.punchline: Captain mentions hundreds of deaths including his wife, but Bender only cares about his ass
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Bender's complete narcissism - tragedy means nothing compared to his vanity

jokes.text: My ass is my soul mate. It even has its own hind-brain. Really? Your butt can think? No. But it can feel. And right now, it's hind-heartbroken!
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his deep connection to his posterior
jokes.punchline: Series of puns: hind-brain, hind-heartbroken, plus the absurd concept of a sentient butt
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay with 'hind' puns and absurd robot anatomy, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Why isn't there a lighthouse to guide us away from the rocks? I don't know, But look out for that lighthouse!
jokes.setup: Leela wondering why there's no lighthouse for navigation
jokes.punchline: Immediately crashes into the lighthouse she wished for
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic reversal with perfect timing - wish fulfilled immediately and disastrously

jokes.text: I suppose you'll be wanting To keep your tooth fillin's. Are ye hurt? No, but we could use a hug.
jokes.setup: Lighthouse keeper assumes they want to keep their dental work (implying he usually takes it)
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent request for comfort instead of material concerns
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - creepy opportunist vs. innocent Fry needing emotional support

jokes.text: That'll be my cup-a-soup. It's been microwavin' longer'n I can remember.
jokes.setup: Bell dings in lighthouse keeper's quarters
jokes.punchline: Reveals extremely long microwaving time due to his isolation-induced time distortion
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing how isolation has warped his sense of time

jokes.text: Found me a whole blue man group once. So you have two jobs? More. I also salvage the wrecks with my bathysphere, And I'm a butcher, If you like blue meat.
jokes.setup: Lighthouse keeper talking about salvaging things that wash ashore
jokes.punchline: Reveals he found the Blue Man Group and now sells their meat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation from salvage to cannibalism, with specific pop culture reference making it absurd

jokes.text: Aye, I'll help ye for a cut of the booty. Now, when you say a cut of the booty... I mean a percentage of the value of the recovered goods.
jokes.setup: Tarquin offers to help for 'a cut of the booty'
jokes.punchline: Bender thinks it's about his literal posterior, Tarquin clarifies it's about treasure
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on multiple meanings of 'booty' given the quest for Bender's rear end

jokes.text: The booty call is at daybreak.
jokes.setup: Arranging when to start the salvage mission
jokes.punchline: Using 'booty call' in maritime context with obvious sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Simple pun on booty call, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Let's not forget that this shipwreck Is also the final resting place of this brave captain. Place your bets. Whee!
jokes.setup: Leela solemnly acknowledging the tragic loss of life in shipwreck
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately treats the captain's skeleton like gambling entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Leela's respect for the dead vs. Bender's callous gambling instincts

jokes.text: If ye like your shoes unmatched and with feet still in 'em, This is your night.
jokes.setup: Storm will wash up debris from shipwrecks
jokes.punchline: Gruesome detail about severed feet still in shoes washing ashore
jokes.explanation: Effectively dark humor with specific macabre details that paint the lighthouse keeper's grisly world

jokes.text: I thought we were near the rocks, But I don't see the lighthouse, So everything is fine and we can relax. All hands on deck For mojitos.
jokes.setup: Ship captain reasoning about navigation safety
jokes.punchline: Dangerously faulty logic leads to ordering drinks right before disaster
jokes.explanation: Good setup for disaster with captain's false confidence and party atmosphere

jokes.text: Your aft-plate. Its fiery gleam saved A ship full of south beach swabbies. Well, I do polish it every day, And I sleep in a chamois diaper.
jokes.setup: Tarquin explaining how Bender's shiny rear end saved a ship
jokes.punchline: Bender's obsessive maintenance routine including sleeping in chamois diapers
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Bender's vanity with appropriately gross specifics

jokes.text: Maybe I have been selfish, Hiding my ass under a bushel. Perhaps, like all great men, My ass has a destiny, And if it does, I can't stand in its way.
jokes.setup: Bender contemplating whether to sacrifice his rear end for the greater good
jokes.punchline: Grandiose speech about his posterior having a noble destiny, parodying heroic sacrifice monologues
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody of heroic sacrifice speeches applied to Bender's vanity about his rear end - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I used to stick my rear out the window. My behind used to make music. Even my ass wouldn't watch that.
jokes.setup: Crew watching movies and trying to cheer up Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender relates every movie title to his missing posterior in increasingly ridiculous ways
jokes.explanation: Good running gag showing Bender's obsession - he relates everything back to his missing rear

jokes.text: Now what say I read you a nice passage From the scripture to help you forget yer old master? 'In the sanctuary, he made two cherubim of olive-wood, each ten cubits high. And one wing measured five cubits, And the other wing measured five cubits; from the tip...'
jokes.setup: Tarquin trying to comfort Bender's rear end with scripture reading
jokes.punchline: The biblical passage happens to be about detailed measurements, paralleling their obsession with body parts
jokes.explanation: Clever choice of biblical text that ironically mirrors the episode's focus on measurements and body parts

jokes.text: Thank you, assie.
jokes.setup: Child saved by the lighthouse beacon (Bender's rear end)
jokes.punchline: Innocent gratitude to 'Assie' - treating the rear end as a heroic character
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that dignifies Bender's sacrifice while maintaining the absurdity of thanking a rear end

jokes.text: Assie? Oh, assie, you came home! You came home.
jokes.setup: Bender's butt returns to him after saving lives
jokes.punchline: Emotional reunion with his own posterior, treating it like a lost pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff to Bender's journey. The nickname 'Assie' makes his posterior feel like a beloved companion returning home.
